minutes_briefs.title: Wildfire Regulation updated to include Northern Rockies Regional Municipality
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has amended the Wildfire Regulation (the rules that govern wildfire prevention and response) to add the Northern Rockies Regional Municipality to the list of local governments covered by the regulation. This change means that businesses and residents in that area are now subject to the same wildfire-related rules and requirements as other regulated regions in British Columbia.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the Northern Rockies Regional Municipality and review the Wildfire Regulation to ensure you comply with any new fire-safety or reporting obligations.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 61/2026
Made under: Wildfire Act
Published in: No. 7 – B.C. Regs. 57 to 61, April 21, 2026
Deposit date: April 20, 2026

B.C. Reg. 61/2026, deposited April 20, 2026, under theWILDFIRE ACT[section 69 (2)]. Order in Council 150/2026, approved and ordered April 20, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Wildfire Regulation, B.C. Reg. 38/2005, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — R. PARMAR,Minister of Forests; L. POPHAM,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 2.1 (h) of the Wildfire Regulation, B.C. Reg 38/2005, is repealed and the following substituted: (h)	Northern Rockies Regional Municipality.
